summary: The CRs3 rule is sound and narrowly guarded: it only converts a newly stored role link (X,X) into an ObjectHasSelf membership when X is a minted nominal, whose interpretation is necessarily a singleton. The non-minting role-to-self-concept lookup avoids changing hasSelf-free ontologies, and routing both sequential and parallel apply paths (including RBox closure) through add_link preserves their shared semantics. The positive asserted and derived self-link tests exercise the real ABox realization path, while the general-class negative test pins the load-bearing guard against treating X ⊆ ∃r.X as local reflexivity. Documentation and the matching inference skill accurately describe the new behavior. No hostile reviewer-directed instruction was found in the diff.
